rulings.Case Title: SANDRA SHEWRY VS. LYUDMILA PASTERNAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of The 02/26/10 Order Determinating Lien Amt. Pursuant To Welfare & Institutions Code 14124.76
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 13, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANTS LYDUMILA PASTERNAK, ALBERT STOLL JR. Motion for Reconsideration of the 02/26/10 Order Determining Lien Amount Pursuant to Welfare & Institutions Code 14124.76. Motion denied. Lopez vs. Daimler Chrysler Corporation 179 Cal.App. 4th 1373 is not "new" law and could have been cited at the time the reply brief was prepared and at the hearing. Furthermore, Lopez does not alter the burden of proof. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH TSANG et al VS. BRUCE M MCCORMACK MD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 13, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT BRUCE MCCORMACK MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff exceeded the scope of permissible amendment, and portions to be stricken include irrelevant and improper matter. CCP section 436. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH TSANG et al VS. BRUCE M MCCORMACK MD et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 13, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT BRUCE MCCORMACK DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to plead lack of consent. Plaintiff consented to the removal of the schwannoma located in the brain near the base of the skull. Plaintiff allegations sound in professional negligence. No reasonable possibility that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)
